sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The exp_mc20 class is a specialization of the event_template and is designed to handle events from the experimental Monte Carlo simulation for Run 3 (MC20). It defines the structure of the event, including the particles and their relationships, and provides methods for building and compiling the event from the input data.

sect1.title: The exp_mc20 Class
sect1.para: The exp_mc20 class inherits from event_template and implements its virtual methods to provide event-specific functionality.

listitem.para: exp_mc20(): The constructor initializes the event by setting its name, defining the data leaves to be read from the input tree, and registering the particle types that are expected in the event.
~exp_mc20(): The destructor cleans up any resources allocated by the event.
event_template* clone(): Creates a new instance of the exp_mc20 event.
void build(element_t* el): Populates the event's data members (event number, pile-up, missing transverse energy, etc.) from the input data element.

void CompileEvent(): This is the core method of the class. It reconstructs the event by linking the various particles together. This includes:
Associating top children with their parent tops.
Linking physics truth objects to their parent tops.
Linking physics detector objects to their parent tops.
Matching detector-level objects (jets, electrons, muons) to their corresponding truth-level physics detector objects.
Vectorizing the particle collections for easier access.

sect2.title: Particle Collections
sect2.para:
The exp_mc20 event contains the following particle collections:
m_tops: A map of top quarks in the event.
m_children: A map of the decay products of the top quarks.
m_physdet: A map of physics detector objects.
m_phystru: A map of physics truth objects.
m_electrons: A map of electrons in the event.
m_muons: A map of muons in the event.
m_jets: A map of jets in the event.

After CompileEvent() is called, these maps are used to populate the following vector collections for easier access:
Tops
TopChildren
PhysicsTruth
PhysicsDetector
Detector (a combined collection of jets, muons, and electrons)
Jets
Leptons (a combined collection of electrons and muons)
